title: cinchona parviflora
intro: Cinchona parviflora, also known as the yellow cinchona, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family. It is a close relative of the Cinchona officinalis, which is the source of the antimalarial drug quinine. The medicinal properties of Cinchona parviflora are attributed to its high content of alkaloids, particularly quinovic acid and quinovic acids derivatives, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Cinchona parviflora include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and malaria, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions.
